title: Field of fractions
content:
In abstract algebra, the field of fractions of an integral domain is the smallest field in which it can be embedded. The construction of the field of fractions is modeled on the relationship between the integral domain of integers and the field of rational numbers. Intuitively, it consists of ratios between integral domain elements. The field of fractions of formula_1 is sometimes denoted by formula_2 or formula_3, and the construction is sometimes called the fraction field, field of quotients, or quotient field of formula_1. All four are in common usage, but are not to be confused with the quotient of a ring by an ideal, which is a quite different concept.

For a commutative ring which is not an integral domain, a related construction is called the localization or ring of quotients. Definition. Given an integral domain and letting formula_5, we define an equivalence relation on formula_6 by letting formula_7 whenever formula_8. We denote the equivalence class of formula_9 by the fraction formula_10. This notion of equivalence is motivated by the rational numbers formula_11, which have the same property with respect to the underlying ring formula_12 of integers. Then the field of fractions is the set formula_13 with addition given by formula_14 and multiplication given by formula_15 One may check that these operations are well defined and that, for any integral domain formula_1, formula_17 is indeed a field.

In particular, for formula_18, the multiplicative inverse of formula_10 is as expected: formula_20. The embedding of formula_1 in formula_2 maps each formula_23 in formula_1 to the fraction formula_25 for any nonzero formula_26 (the equivalence class is independent of the choice formula_27). This is modeled on the identity formula_28. The field of fractions of formula_1 is characterized by the following universal property: There is a categorical interpretation of this construction. Let formula_35 be the category of integral domains and injective ring maps.

The functor from formula_35 to the category of fields which takes every integral domain to its fraction field and every homomorphism to the induced map on fields (which exists by the universal property) is the left adjoint of the inclusion functor from the category of fields to formula_35. Thus the category of fields (which is a full subcategory) is a reflective subcategory of formula_35. A multiplicative identity is not required for the role of the integral domain; this construction can be applied to any nonzero commutative rng formula_1 with no nonzero zero divisor s. The embedding is given by formula_40 for any nonzero formula_41. Generalizations. Localization.

For any commutative ring formula_1 and any multiplicative set formula_49 in formula_1, the localization formula_51 is the commutative ring consisting of fraction s with formula_53 and formula_54, where now formula_55 is equivalent to formula_56 if and only if there exists formula_57 such that formula_58. Two special cases of this are notable: Note that it is permitted for formula_49 to contain 0, but in that case formula_51 will be the trivial ring. Semifield of fractions. The semifield of fractions of a commutative semiring with no zero divisor s is the smallest semifield in which it can be embedded. The elements of the semifield of fractions of the commutative semiring formula_1 are equivalence class es written as with formula_74 and formula_75 in formula_1.
